economic_notes: 41% of revenue from government subsidies, 43% from fishing revenues. Subsidiaries: Société de développement Wulustuk inc., Érablière Malécite S.E.C., Société de gestion Toku. Investments in Les Crevettiers Marinard inc., 9375-8795 Québec inc. (100%), Énergie Éolienne Bas-Saint-Laurent SENC (10%), 9060-7375 Québec inc. (16.67%). Fishing rights (shrimp and crab) are significant assets.
notes: Fiscal year end March 31, 2020. French-language statements. Province QC. Three consolidated subsidiaries: Société de développement Wulustuk inc., Érablière Malécite S.E.C., Société de gestion Toku. Large deferred revenue of $6,498,678 mostly from DFO (Pêches et Océans Canada) funding for fisheries access. Prior year included $2,751,641 in specific claims from AANC which inflated prior year revenue. Retroactive adjustment of $42,586 to opening accumulated surplus. COVID-19 subsequent event noted. No remuneration schedule included in extracted text. ISC funding identified as AANC $1,491,911 (operating); total government transfers include federal $2,659,518 and provincial $469,921 = $3,129,439. OSR calculated as $7,655,466 - $3,129,439 = $4,526,027 (primarily commercial fishing activities). Fishing rights carried at $2,508,180 net book value as intangible asset.
